name: Criteria for A-stability
statement_text:
Let (A, b, c) denote a Runge–Kutta method. Then its stability
function is given by
                                    det (I + z(1b − A))
                           R(z) =                       .
                                         det(I − zA)


                               40i




                          α

                          α                            50




                               −40i


          Figure 350(i)   A(α) stability region for the method (350c)
proof_text:
Proof. Because a rank 1 s × s matrix uv has characteristic polynomial
det(Iw − uv ) = ws−1 (w − v u), a matrix of the form I + uv has characteristic
polynomial (w−1)s−1 (w−1−v u) and determinant of the form 1+v u. Hence,
                                  
          det I + z1b (I − zA)−1 = 1 + zb (I − zA)−1 1 = R(z).

We now note that
                                               
              I + z(1b − A) = I + z1b (I − zA)−1 (I − zA),

so that
                   det (I + z(1b − A)) = R(z) det(I − zA).
proof_latex:
\begin{proof}
Because a rank~1 $s \times s$ matrix $uv^{\top}$ has characteristic polynomial
\[
\det(I w - uv^{\top}) = w^{s-1}(w - v^{\top}u),
\]
a matrix of the form $I + uv^{\top}$ has characteristic polynomial $(w-1)^{s-1}(w-1-v^{\top}u)$ and determinant of the form $1+v^{\top}u$. Hence,
\[
\det\Bigl(I + z\mathbf{1}b^{\top}(I - zA)^{-1}\Bigr) = 1 + zb^{\top}(I - zA)^{-1}\mathbf{1} = R(z).
\]
We now note that
\[
I + z(\mathbf{1}b^{\top} - A) = \Bigl(I + z\mathbf{1}b^{\top}(I - zA)^{-1}\Bigr)(I - zA),
\]
so that
\[
\det\bigl(I + z(\mathbf{1}b^{\top} - A)\bigr) = R(z) \det(I - zA).
\]
Now write the stability function of a Runge--Kutta method as the ratio of two polynomials
\[
R(z) = \frac{N(z)}{D(z)}
\]
and define the $E$-polynomial by
\[
E(y) = D(iy)D(-iy) - N(iy)N(-iy).
\]
\end{proof}
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
The stability function \(R(z)\) of a Runge--Kutta method with coefficients \((A,b,c)\) is a rational function.  Lemma~351A gives an alternative expression for \(R(z)\).

variables.name: (A, b, c)
variables.definition: Runge–Kutta method with coefficient matrix A, weight vector b, and node vector c

equations.content: Rational function for stability function (referenced earlier in the text, but not explicitly shown in the provided excerpt; it is the general form of R(z) for a Runge–Kutta method)

preamble: The text discusses stability properties of Runge–Kutta methods, introducing A-stability and A(α)-stability. The stability function R(z) is a rational function characterizing the method's stability. Lemma 351A provides an alternative expression for R(z) in terms of the method's coefficients.
